content.query:
--Create Dedicated SQL Pool
-- Goto Manage -> SQL Pools -> Dedicated SQL Pool

CREATE SCHEMA bank_schema;

Create table dedsqlwh.bank_schema.bank_txns_plain
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
);

--Create a table under Database.Schema.tablename
--Database/dwh is container of all associated objects such as tables, indexes, view, sp, mat views etc.,
--Schema is a subdivision of a Database for managing subject area wise objects. eg. hrschema, empschema, devschema, testschema, prodschema.
--What happens behind the scene? DW100c Computes taken the query and created metadata in SQLserver Warehouse under DW100c and 
-- internally under some storage (AZ) it creates some folders (behind the scene, not in our control).
Create table dedsqlwh.bank_schema.bank_txns
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN,  -- distributes data evenly across all nodes, using spark we use what function? repartition(4)
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX  -- columnar data, compression, Segmented, Batch, PDO (Predicate & Projection) & faster CRUD/ACID -- Equivalent to using what file format ? delta(parquet)
);

--supports all CRUD(create,read,update,delete) operations or ACID Transactions (not supported in External tables)

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;


INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1001, '2026-04-01', 'CUST001', 1500.50, 'T. Nagar', 'Chennai');
INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1002, '2026-04-02', 'CUST002', 2750.00, 'Adyar', 'Chennai');
INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1003, '2026-04-02', 'CUST003', 500.25, 'Jayanagar', 'Bangalore');
INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1004, '2026-04-03', 'CUST001', 3200.00, 'T. Nagar', 'Chennai');
INSERT INTO bank_schema.bank_txns (txnid, txndate, custid, amt, branch, region)
VALUES 
(1005, '2026-04-05', 'CUST004', 120.00, 'Hitech City', 'Hyderabad');


select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;

--update
UPDATE bank_schema.bank_txns set amt = 2000 where branch = 'Jayanagar';

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;


--delete
DELETE FROM bank_schema.bank_txns where txnid=1005;

select * from bank_schema.bank_txns;

--HASH
Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_hash
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,  
    DISTRIBUTION = HASH(region)  -- distributes data across all nodes using hash algorithm applied on the respective Regions, relate with spark? repartition(4,col(region) )
    --Benifit is , if an analytics team is analysing the sales in NY region, then without disturbing other nodes, analytics can be made simple.
    --number value of NY = 10 % 4 = 2 (all NY region data will go to node number 2)
    --number value of CA = 11 % 4 = 3 (all CA region data will go to node number 3)
    --number value of TX = 15 % 4 = 3 (all TX region data will go to node number 3)
);


--replicate
Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_replicate
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX, 
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE  --Store small tables data across all nodes, use this feature only if the table size is small and it undergoes frequent join or lookup.
    --Equivalent in Spark? broadcast(smalldata) or broadcast join
);


--heap index
Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_heap_indx
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    HEAP, -- Default storage, apply no strategies data storage without using any clustering/sorting/indexing mechanism to enable faster write operations
    --Where we can apply this? If I create tables in Bronze layer (as is data) and I don't enable frequent analysis on this data.
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE
);

--clustered index
Create table bank_schema.bank_txns_cluster_indx
(
    txnid int,
    txndate date,
    custid varchar(20),
    amt float,
    branch varchar(50),
    region varchar(50)
)
WITH
(
    CLUSTERED INDEX(region), --Clustered storage based on region for faster filtering based on region by regional managers/marketing team, Sorted, Small datasets, we can relate with Spark ? df.write.partitionBy("region") 
    DISTRIBUTION = REPLICATE --Every node data stored like this /rootdir/region=NY/100 ny data.txt,  /rootdir/region=NJ/200 nj data.txt
);

select * from INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES;
